title: General preparation of liposomes using probe-tip sonication
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4mrsu","text":"This method outlines a general approach for preparing liposomes using probe-tip sonication.  The method has been optimized for the preparation of pure DPPC liposomes on a 25-mg scale and may require modifications as the quantity of lipid is altered or upon addition of other lipids and small molecules. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"78ha6","text":"Allow lipids to reach room temperature prior to weighing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"a2qpj","text":"Work in the hood if you are using lipid dissolved in chloroform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d2js6","text":"Ear protection should be used with a sonicating apparatus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8hjo3","text":"1,2-dipalmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (DPPC) lipids can be purchased commercially from Avanti Lipids either as a dried powder or dissolved in chloroform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":16,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"800s2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9g25v","text":"The lipid powder can be dissolved in ethanol or hexane as an alternative to chloroform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Lipids are typically stored at -20 °C and should be allowed to reach room temperature prior to working with them. For DPPC powder, carefully weigh out 25 mg on a clean analytical balance using clean, ungloved hands (to minimize static)
Carefully transfer the lipid to a clean 2.0 mL glass vial. Add 2.0 mL of (0.2 um-filtered) buffer. (20 mM HEPES, 100 mM NaCl, pH 7.4)
Samples were vortexed to mix and hydrate the lipid powder or dried film (if prepared from a chloroform solution lipids should be dried to a film under a stream of nitrogen gas).

To suspend the lipids more homogeneously and remove large particulates the mixture can be sonicated using a probe-tip sonicator (Fisher Scientific, Hampton, NH) set to 20% duty cycle with a pulse time of 2 seconds followed by a rest period of 2 seconds for a total sonication time of 2 minutes .

To prepare liposomes from this mixture, the cycle in step 4 should be repeated 3 additional times for a total of 4 cycles at 2 minutes total sonication time per cycle. Total liposome preparation time is 8 minutes. The rest period is important to avoid excessive heating of the sample. Temperature should always be monitored closely.
Samples were centrifuged using a standard benchtop microcentrifuge at for 3 minutes to remove residual titanium particles from the sonicator probe tip and un-reconstituted lipids.
Carefully remove the supernatant and transfer to a clean 2.0 mL Eppendorf tube.
Samples can be stored at 4 °C for up to 24 hours. An additional centrifugation step should be carried out on samples that have been stored to remove any precipitated lipid.
creator: Monica Rieth
